message:
fix(fcp-mesh): coordinator must not panic on u64::MAX fencing token from adversarial peer

An adversarial or buggy peer reporting fencing_token = u64::MAX in an
expired lease panics the coordinator on the next legitimate acquire()
call. The earlier defensive patch (commit 1233c361) addressed
rebase_next_seq but next_fencing_token retained a checked_add(1).expect
that fires whenever next_seq reaches u64::MAX — which the saturating
rebase pins it to.

Fix: change next_fencing_token to return Option<u64>, refusing to hand
out a token when the space is exhausted. acquire() maps None to
AcquireOutcome::Conflict with reason "fencing token space exhausted"
so callers receive a structured error instead of a panicked process.

Regression test covers the expired-poisoned-lease path that the prior
test (active-poisoned-lease) does not exercise, since the Denied
branch of acquire bypasses next_fencing_token entirely.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
